entry.title: Re: Session management and custom Field mappings in CDC
entry.content:
Regarding the second question: How does Commerce know the CDC session has expired?

Short answer: It doesn't get notified — it detects it indirectly through the browser cookie.

There is no server-to-server push notification from CDC to Commerce/Hybris. The mechanism is purely cookie-based:

  • CDC's Web SDK deletes the GLT cookie when the session expires
  • GigyaLoginFilter running in Commerce/Hybris checks on every request: "Is the GLT cookie still there?"
  • If the cookie is gone → logout is triggered

That's the entire detection logic — just a null cookie check. 

This is a known trade-off of cookie-based session sync. It works well for normal browser usage but has a gap for idle sessions or headless/API clients that don't send cookies.

entry.title: Re: Recording one characteristic at multiple fixed measur...
entry.content: Hello Memem, Unfortunately, I don't think this solution would be sufficient for our scenario. Suppose I have a delivery consisting of 3 pieces of material. In that case, it won't be possible to collect 5 samples for the Diameter characteristic, even if I assign a sampling procedure requiring 5 samples to that MIC. SAP will only allow me to inspect the maximum number of available pieces, so only 3 result fields will be available instead of 5. Do you know if there is any way to use Inspection Points for only a single MIC? I tried using Free Inspection Points in the inspection plan header, but unfortunately this forces me to change the sampling procedure for all MICs in the inspection plan, not just the Diameter characteristic. I'm wondering if there is any other standard way to handle this requirement.

entry.title: SAP RDP: ES Plastic Tax for non-EU imports
entry.content:
Dear community,

I would like some clarification on the extract of the report documentation from SAP Responsible Design and Production below. My understanding is that goods receipts from suppliers outside of EU are not relevant for the Spanish Plastic Tax (only for inventory balancing). In the report documentation seems to be a mistake saying that the supplier of the non-EU import should be from a EU member state (see red text below). Could you please confirm how the Spanish Plastic Tax considers imports from non-EU countries?

Import Non-EU (IMPORT)

This logistic process is used with the logistic processes GR_ST_IMP and GR_PO_IMP to display the reporting data in the Analyze Reporting Data app and for the packaging inventory balance.

No

Yes

  • The Posting Date Date must be within the validity period of the report configuration and reporting period.

  • The Plant Country ISO Code must be Spain.

  • For logistic process GR_ST_IMP, the Issuing or Receiving Plant Country ISO Code must be an European Union member state other than Spain.

  • For logistic process GR_PO_IMP, the Supplier Country must be a European Union member state, the Supplier Country ISO Code must not be Spain, and supplier is not liable for plastic tax.

    • You can check the existing goods with IncoTerms and whether the supplier is liable for plastic tax in the Configuration Data app.

  • The QuantityInBaseUnit attribute is maintained as described in Material Documents.

Best regards,

Jessy

entry.title: Re: Session management and custom Field mappings in CDC
entry.content:
Hi @Ritumbhara 

About the 3600s configuration in the Spartacus CDC library setup, this hard-coded value will be passed to CDC from the JS storefront application. 

So, your understanding is correct — it's the session duration in seconds.

This value is the sessionExpiration config for CDC, either passed to the CDC showScreenSet() call when rendering a login screen or passed to the CDC accounts.login() API for headless login, and it controls how long the CDC/Gigya session token stays valid after login.

When a user logs in (either through a CDC Screen Set or a direct CDC API call), this value is passed to the CDC Web SDK to set the session lifetime. So with 3600s, the user's CDC session will expire after 1 hour of inactivity or elapsed time, depending on the session type.

Is it related to session management? — Yes, directly. This is the value that feeds into the session lifecycle we discussed:

  • It sets the CDC session duration
  • Once it expires, the GLT cookie gets cleared
  • Which then triggers the Commerce/Hybris logout on the next request (as explained in the previous question)

You can configure this per site in your Spartacus CDC config, and it falls back to 3600s (1 hour) as the default if not explicitly set.

entry.title: Re: Setup a pricing configuration for aggregated usage ch...
entry.content:
Hi, 
the subscription id is not a standard field for pricing and is therefore not available in the pricing context. See the list of standard fields here. Also, usage record item fields are only available for individually rated usage records, not for aggregated ones. 

Basically there are two options (see Customized Pricing ) 

  1. customize the price of the subscription using an overridable field
  2. make use of a custom reference to influence pricing 

The second option is better suited if you have an attribute which influences the calculated price for multiple subscriptions in the same way. The first option is probably the one you are looking for. 
The automatic pricing check for rate plans in the product app shows the information in case additional attributes are used to calculate a price, because typically the actual subscription data is not available then. 

Please check out also these blogs:

  • Pricing based on custom attributes with SAP Subscription Billing
  • Customizing the Pricing of a Subscription

entry.title: Creating an SAP SD Billing Document Using a BAPI in SAP Build and Accessing It Through 'Joule Work'
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

Recently, I was working on a requirement to create an SD billing document in SAP S/4HANA using SAP Build Process Automation and make it accessible through the Joule Work mobile app.

Due to some internal requirements & technical restrictions, the only option available to me was to use the BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE BAPI to create the billing document.

I am sharing the following demo which I created in the test environment. To use this BAPI in SAP Build, there are two main configurations required:

1. Map the virtual host to the internal host in SAP Cloud Connector using the RFC protocol, and expose the required BAPI or RFC function module.

2. Create a destination of type RFC in the SAP BTP Cockpit.

Let's first see how Cloud to On-Premises mapping in Cloud Connector looks like:

We also need to enable the required resources, such as the BAPIs and function modules that we want to consume in SAP Build. The RFC_FUNCTION_SEARCH function module enables the search for function modules from SAP Build.



And this is how the destination in BTP looks:



The virtual message server is configured in the Cloud Connector and mapped to the internal SAP S/4HANA host, and logon group information is available in SMLG.



Logon group can be found here :



Now that the Cloud Connector and BTP destination configurations are complete, let’s log in to SAP Build and see how we can create an action using BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE.

Select RFC's, BAPI's:



All the BAPIs & RFCs from backend will be available to use. We will go ahead and look for BAPI/RFC we'd like to use, and create a new project.



The action is now available in Connectors -> Actions, and can be used in our build projects.



Using this action, I created a custom process that can be triggered to generate a billing document by entering the reference sales document number.



Let's see it in action;
Assume we have below sales order, for which we'd like to generate a billing document.



Let’s now access the custom process we created. I have added the form URL to SAP Build Work Zone for easy access.





Since I added an email notification step to the process, we receive an email once the billing document is generated successfully.



Let's validated the generated billing document number in the system.



To access SAP Build Work Zone applications and tiles in the Joule mobile app, open Settings, scan the QR code, and complete the authentication process.





As we can see below, all our SAP Build Work Zone applications are now accessible through the Joule Work mobile app as well.





Thanks for reading!

Stay tuned for more demos on AI agents using Joule Studio, n8n, and automation in SAP Build.

entry.title: Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1 is out
entry.content:
With the latest release of Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1, the app provides exciting features for both, the New Experience (Engagement Layer), as well as the SAP Build Work Zone Experience – formally called SAP Mobile Start.

Let me start with the features that are valid for both experiences.
Based on customer feedback, the app allows you now to customise the Support Section on the Settings screen. This allows you to add additional information for your users, in case they have any support needs. Administrators can easily add information, such as phone numbers, email addresses, and websites for further end user support. Once configured, the info is displayed in the Profile and Settings screen as well as during the onboarding process. The feature can be added and configured via SAP Mobile Services or via Mobile Device Management (MDM). Please also check our SAP Help page for details.

We also have updated the supported languages for SAP Joule Work and added: Greek, Croatian, Indonesian, Romanian, Ukrainian, and Vietnamese.

Now, let us have a look at the features that are relevant for the SAP Build Work Zone Experience.
First and most important, you can now directly access workspaces from SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition - via a dedicated screen that is available in the navigation bar. This ensures that you get direct access to your most needed content from anywhere in the app. In case you work with many workspaces, you can easily filter them, for instance if you only want to see your favourites – or search for them. As soon as this feature got enabled by the admin,  the SAP Build Work Zone Workspaces screen will be visible.  

Screenshot: SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition – Workspaces

Second, users can now change the language of external SAP Build Work Zone tiles, like news and to-dos directly inside the app, from the settings screen.

Now, let us have a look at the new features for our Joule Work New Experience.
As introduced in my last blog “Joule Work, mobile app is here”, Spaces are one of the key elements of the New Experience. Lets recap: Spaces are dynamic, intent-driven workspaces for more complex business problems. They are composed in real time to bring the right data from different systems, tools, and context together for a specific goal, freeing people from working in multiple applications and passive UIs. With the new release, you can now pin your favourite spaces to the top of the spaces list, what will make it much easier for you to access your favourite content. To do so, just do a long tap on the space that you want to pin and the menu appears. Here, you can easily rename your space, delete it or pin/ unpin it. Joule Work New Experience is currently available within an Early Adapter Care program.

 Screenshot: Pin favourite Spaces to the top of the list

I hope you will enjoy these features in the Joule Work mobile app.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Re: Standard API required for Manage Workforce App
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

You can use the API "Replicate Basic Master Data for Workforce from External HR Systems" for fetching the data for the Worker maintained in the application Manage Workforce.

The below nodes contains the required data for the respective purpose:

WorkAgreementInformation | SAP Help Portal -> It stores the data for the Worker Type in the field PersonWorkAgreementType and the Work agreement Ids in the field (PersonWorkAgreementUUID)

ValidityPeriod | SAP Help Portal -> It stores the start date and the end date for the Work agreements which maps to the start and the end date of the Worker Type as well.

 

Thanks and Regards,
Moumita.

entry.title: Re: Joule A2A: Propagate the User Identity from Joule into S/4HANA
entry.content: Hi @M_M_J, you are on a good track here. As the title suggests this is mainly for Public Cloud. The pattern for PCE and OnPrem are similar in a sense that a) you need to activate the ODATA Services first https://me.sap.com/notes/0002595454 here you just use the classical "/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE" instead of the Fiori Apps b) ensure connectivity via the Cloud Connector https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/scenario-cloud-to-on-premise and c) configure the token flow from Joule to the PCE system https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/configure-principal-propagation-via-ias-token There are some slight differences - while S4 Public is using Oauth2SAMLBearer - we use the PrincipalPropagation Destination Type here. In deed I am looking to create something like this in another blog soon, but currently I am still awaiting getting my hands on a system 🙂

entry.title: [SAP Partners and Colleagues only] Save the date for the Product Expert Training H2 2026!
entry.content:
We are excited to invite you to save the date for the Product Expert Training (PET) H2 2026 flagship event, starting on September 28 and running for approximately three weeks.

Following the launch of our evolved PET format in H1, the H2 program will bring together SAP product experts and the partner community through a combination of onsite and virtual sessions focused on innovation, implementation insights, and collaboration across SAP Autonomous Suite.

 

Visit the landing page

 

Save the date

 

 

Powered by Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success

The Product Expert Training program is driven by the Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success organization, bringing together product expertise and enablement across a broad SAP Autonomous Suite portfolio.

PET content covers a wide range of solution areas, including:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public and Private Edition),
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM),
  • Cloud LoBs,
  • Data and Platform,
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP),
  • etc.

Through PET, SAP product experts share strategic insights, innovations, implementation experiences, and end-to-end business process knowledge to support partner success across the SAP ecosystem.

 

Event format at a glance

📍Week 1
Hybrid onsite experience in St. Leon-Rot, Germany, combined with virtual participation opportunities.

💻Week 2
Fully virtual expert sessions.

💻Week 3
Fully virtual expert sessions.

 

What to expect from the onsite event?

During the event, partners can look forward to:

Strategic insights

  • Executive updates
  • GTM and AI strategy
  • Product direction and innovation outlook


Product and project insights

  • End-to-end process sessions
  • Implementation experiences and best practices
  • Innovation deep dives from SAP product experts


Partner exchange

  • Networking opportunities
  • Community interaction
  • Cross-line-of-business collaboration


The event is designed to support continuous learning, strengthen implementation expertise, and foster exchange across the SAP partner ecosystem.

Detailed agenda information and registration links will follow soon. We look forward to welcoming you to PET H2 2026!

 

Continue learning with PET H1 2026

While preparing for the H2 flagship event, PET H1 2026 is still fully ongoing.

Every Friday until mid July, SAP product experts continue to publish new standalone on-demand videos covering:

  • Product insights and innovations
  • End-to-end business processes
  • Implementation experiences and best practices
  • Strategic cross topics across the whole suite


The videos are available on SAP Learning and designed to help partners stay up to date through short, focused learning sessions that can be consumed anytime and at your own pace.

Check out my blog post to explore the latest videos across various end-to-end solution processes and cross topics.

entry.title: Re: @sap/cds-rfc: RFC_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE (NIECONN_BROK...
entry.content:
@rakeshr16 

I have run in hybrid mode with the following settings:

cds bind -2 <destination instance>

Create .cdsrc-private.json.

    "abaptrialRFC": {
      "credentials": {
        "destinationBack": "<destination name>", 
        "connectivity_subaccount": "<subaccount ID>", 
        "connectivity_proxy_host": "localhost",
        "connectivity_proxy_port": "20001",
        "ashost": "<ashost in destination>",
        "client": "<client in destination>",
        "sysnr": "<sysnr in in destination>",
        "connectivity_location_id": "<Location ID in destination>",
        "user": "<ABAP server login user>",
        "passwd": "<ABAP server login password>"
      }
    }

Then execute:

cds watch --profile hybrid

entry.title: Re: PO Print Output – Chinese Material Description Showin...
entry.content:
This issue is almost always caused by font and language settings in the output form, not by the material master itself. When SAP prints “###” instead of Chinese characters, it means the system cannot render the Unicode text with the font currently used in the PO layout.

The fix is purely form and font configuration. Update your PO output form to use a Unicode-capable font and ensure the device type supports Chinese. That will resolve the “###” issue and display the material description correctly.

entry.title: Re: GCS JWT token generation - SAP CPI neo - PKIX path bu...
entry.content: Hi TomXing, Thanks for the reply. We validated trust using the CPI Neo TLS Connectivity Test with ‘Valid Server Certificate Required’ enabled. The test fails with a PKIX error, confirming that the Google OAuth endpoint is not trusted by the CPI Neo runtime, even though the required DigiCert root certificates(gts root r1 & wr2) are present.

entry.content: @MathiasL The side-by-side approach is the recommended option. It offers better availability and scalability while abstracting the underlying infrastructure from end users, which helps reduce operational complexity.Moving forward, this will be the only supported approach, as it is a well-established and standardized architecture.

entry.title: SAP BTP - CI - EventSmartKafka - Custom Kafka Adapter From .ESA v1.1.0 - Validated Runtime - Part IV
entry.content:
EventSmartKafka is an open-source custom Kafka Consumer Sender Adapter for SAP BTP Integration Suite / SAP Cloud Integration, built with SAP ADK.

 

Part ISAP BTP - CI - How I Built an Open-Source Enterprise Custom Kafka Adapter from Scratch - Part I
Part IISAP BTP - CI - Building Open-Source Custom Adapter EventSmartKafka from Zero with SAP ADK - Part II
Part IIISAP BTP - CI - EventSmartKafka - Custom Kafka Adapter From .ESA - Validated Runtime - Part III

This Part IV article covers the final Consumer release: what was added, what was fixed, what was deliberately removed, and what 3,000,000 messages across 4 simultaneous listeners on a SAP BTP Trial proved.

After this release, the Consumer side is closed. The next chapter is the Producer adapter.

"The OSGi classloader does not care about your port. It creates a new world every time you redeploy. The old ServerSocket stays alive, holding the address, invisible to the new classloader. That was the relay bug. And it took exactly one static Map to cause it, and exactly one System property to fix it." — Ricardo Viana, 2026

1. What Changed From v1.0.0-RC1 to v1.1.0

Part III documented RC1: installation, security profiles, SOCKS5 relay and the first 1.5M message validation.

v1.1.0 is the closing release of the Consumer chapter. It adds mTLS, fixes the OSGi relay port lock permanently, corrects dangerous defaults, and validates 3,000,000 messages under parallel load.

 

AreaRC1v1.1.0
mTLSRoadmap✅Validated
OSGi Relay port lockKnown issue✅Fixed permanently
OAuth2Roadmap❌Removed — see Section 6
Infinite retry loopsPresent✅Fixed
TIMESEEK read-allBroken (commit failed)✅Fixed
Stress validation1.5M messages✅3M messages, 0 failures
Total validated1.5M✅4.5M across all cycles

2. mTLS — Mutual TLS Client Certificate Authentication

mTLS in Kafka means the client presents its own certificate during the TLS handshake. The broker validates the client certificate against a trusted CA. No SASL credentials. No username/password. Certificate identity only.

In SAP CPI the certificate chain is stored in the Keystore as a Key Pair — a .p12 file containing the private key and the signed client certificate. The adapter reads it via the mtlsKeyAlias field.

// SdiaKafkaSslConfigurator.java
props.put("security.protocol", "SSL");
props.put("ssl.keystore.location",  resolvedKeystorePath);
props.put("ssl.keystore.password",  keystorePassword);
props.put("ssl.keystore.type",      "PKCS12");
props.put("ssl.key.password",       keyPassword);

The broker-side configuration (ssl.client.auth=required) is what enforces mTLS — the broker rejects any connection that does not present a valid client certificate signed by the trusted CA.

What Was Validated

  • mTLS via Cloud Connector On-Premise (SOCKS5 tunnel)
  • mTLS direct Internet (Cloud broker)
  • Self-signed CA with private truststore
  • Proper CA-signed certificate chain
  • CPI Keystore Key Pair import (.p12)

What mTLS Does NOT Need

One important UI decision in v1.1.0: when Authentication = mTLS, the Broker CA field disappears. This is intentional. mTLS uses the client .p12 for both the private key and the certificate chain. The broker CA trust is derived from the issuer chain inside that same .p12. There is no separate CA alias needed.

3. The OSGi Relay Bug — Root Cause and Fix

This was the most technically complex bug in the entire project. And the most instructive.

The Symptom

After redeploy, the adapter failed with:

Address already in use
The local CPI relay port is already held by another adapter/runtime instance

Restarting SAP Cloud Connector did not help. The port was not held by Kafka. It was not held by Cloud Connector. It was held by the CPI JVM itself — by a ServerSocket from the previous bundle classloader.

The Root Cause

// RC1 — the broken implementation
private static final Map<Integer, ServerSocket> ACTIVE_RELAYS = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();

A static field in Java belongs to the classloader, not the JVM. When OSGi creates a new bundle classloader on redeploy, the static Map is fresh and empty. But the ServerSocket from the previous classloader is still alive — OSGi does not garbage-collect open sockets on bundle stop unless the code explicitly closes them.

The new classloader tries to bind the same port. Fails. The operator sees Address already in use.

The Fix

// v1.1.0 — JVM-wide registry via System properties
private static final String RELAY_REGISTRY_KEY = "sdia.kafka.relay.";

public static void register(int port, ServerSocket socket) {
    System.setProperty(RELAY_REGISTRY_KEY + port,
        String.valueOf(System.identityHashCode(socket)));
}

public static ServerSocket findExisting(int port) {
    // Uses reflection to locate the socket across classloader boundary
    String key = RELAY_REGISTRY_KEY + port;
    if (System.getProperty(key) != null) {
        // Probe the port — if reachable as relay, reuse it
        return probeAndReuse(port);
    }
    return null;
}

System.setProperty is JVM-wide. It survives classloader replacement. The new bundle can find the old relay registration, probe it, verify it is still functional as a relay, and reuse it instead of trying to bind again.

Behavior After Fix

ScenarioRC1v1.1.0
Same classloader redeployWorksWorks
New classloader (OSGi bundle update)Address already in useRelay reused via JVM registry
Port held by foreign processFailsFails with clear message
Legacy unregistered zombie relayFailsPassively reused if reachable

No port changes required. No Cloud Connector restarts required. Clean redeploy every time.

4. Other Fixes — Infinite Loops and Defaults

Infinite Retry Loops

RC1 had a category of bugs where wrong configuration caused endless retry loops instead of stopping cleanly.

Examples:

  • Wrong broker address → metadata timeout loop
  • Missing Security Material → authentication failure loop
  • SOCKS5 tunnel failure → silent reconnect loop

v1.1.0 applies a strict no-retry policy on any fatal initialization failure. The adapter sets nonRetryableConfigurationFailure = true, logs the root cause with a structured error message, and stops. No loop. No zombie. Redeploy required to resume.

Wrong Defaults Fixed

ParameterRC1 Defaultv1.1.0Why
autoOffsetResetearliestlatestSafer for production — don't replay entire topic on new group
errorHandlingStop on ErrorSkip Failed MessageMore forgiving default for first-time users
maxPollRecords1050Better throughput without overwhelming CPI

5. TIMESEEK — Two Modes, Two Fixes

TIMESEEK is the feature that lets operators recover specific messages by timestamp without changing the consumer group ID or reprocessing the entire topic.

v1.1.0 fixed two separate bugs.

Fix 1 — Single Record Mode

RC1 behavior:

seek(timestamp) → assign() → poll() → process ✅ → commitSync(original_offset) ❌
"group has already rebalanced"

The assign() call removes the consumer from the group. The group coordinator sees the consumer leave, triggers rebalance, assigns partitions to other members. When commitSync tries to restore the original offset, the group has already moved on.

Fix: when in assignMode, skip the offset restore entirely. The group offset was never changed by the recovery seek — no restore needed.

Fix 2 — Read All From Timestamp

RC1 used the same broken unsubscribe/assign pattern for read-all mode, causing every offset commit to fail.

Fix: read-all mode now uses ConsumerRebalanceListener.onPartitionsAssigned. The consumer stays in the subscribe() group. The seek is applied inside the callback when Kafka assigns partitions. Commits work normally.

kafkaConsumer.subscribe(topicEntries, new ConsumerRebalanceListener() {
    @Override
    public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {
        if (pendingSubscribeSeekTimestampMs > 0L) {
            // Apply seek here — consumer is still in the group
            Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestamps = ...
            Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsets =
                kafkaConsumer.offsetsForTimes(timestamps);
            for (TopicPartition tp : partitions) {
                OffsetAndTimestamp oat = offsets.get(tp);
                if (oat != null) kafkaConsumer.seek(tp, oat.offset());
            }
            pendingSubscribeSeekTimestampMs = -1L;
        }
    }
});

6. OAuth2 Removed — Why

RC1 planned OAuth2/OAUTHBEARER as a roadmap item. v1.1.0 removes it entirely from the UI and code. This deserves a direct explanation.

The implementation was done. SdiaKafkaOAuthCallbackHandler, SdiaKafkaOAuthTokenFetcher, SdiaKafkaOAuthEndpointHolder — all written, zero Jackson dependency, custom HTTP token fetch with cache and TTL.

The testing was impossible. After spending days trying to validate OAuth2 against Confluent Cloud, Redpanda Cloud and Aiven:

  • Confluent Cloud OAuth2 requires Standard plan — not available on Trial
  • Redpanda Cloud OAuth2 requires paid plan
  • Aiven OAuth2 requires external Identity Provider configuration not available on Trial

The only viable local option was Keycloak. But the apache/kafka:4.1.2 image does not include OAuthBearerValidatorCallbackHandler in its classpath — the class simply does not exist at runtime.

The decision: ship a validated, working adapter without OAuth2 rather than ship untested OAuth2 that could silently misbehave in production. The code is removed from UI but the architecture is documented. When a funded test environment is available, OAuth2 can be re-introduced cleanly.

OAuth2 for Kafka is enterprise-grade and requires enterprise infrastructure to validate properly. That is not available to an independent solo researcher on a Trial tenant.

7. Parallel Consumers

v1.1.0 adds parallelConsumers — a new field that creates N consumer instances sharing the same group.id. Kafka distributes partitions among them automatically via standard group rebalance.

// SdiaKafkaMultiConsumer.java
public final class SdiaKafkaMultiConsumer extends DefaultConsumer {
    private final List<SdiaKafkaConsumer> consumers;

    public SdiaKafkaMultiConsumer(SdiaKafkaEndpoint endpoint,
                                   Processor processor, int count) {
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
            consumers.add(new SdiaKafkaConsumer(endpoint, processor));
        }
    }

    @Override
    protected void doStart() throws Exception {
        for (SdiaKafkaConsumer c : consumers) c.start();
    }
}

Rules enforced by the adapter:

  • parallelConsumers > 1 + explicit Partition → fail-fast error. These modes are mutually exclusive.
  • parallelConsumers > number of partitions → warning logged. Idle consumers waste threads.
  • Effective parallelism is always bounded by partition count.

8. The Final Stress Test — 3,000,000 Messages

Sunday morning. SAP BTP Trial. 4 listeners running simultaneously.

 

MetricResult
Total messages3,000,000
Total failed0
Avg throughput1,825 msg/s
Avg bandwidth1.88 MiB/s
Total data3.023 GiB
Elapsed00:27:23
CPI Completed3,000,023
CPI Failed0
CPI Retry0

What was running simultaneously:

ListenerPortProtocolAuth
NONEEXT19108PLAINTEXTNone
SASLEXT19109SASL_PLAINTEXTPLAIN + SCRAM-256 + SCRAM-512
SASLSSLEXT19110SASL_SSLPLAIN + SCRAM-256 + SCRAM-512
MTLSEXT19111SSLmTLS client certificate

Avro payload distribution:

  • 53 B — 2,998,000 messages (small event payloads)
  • 1 MB — 1,000 messages (large Avro records)
  • 2 MB — 1,000 messages (oversized Avro shipment records)

4 Cloud Connector tunnels active. OSGi Relay handling redeploys cleanly. Zero failures across 3 GiB of data.

Combined with the RC1 1.5M validation: 4.5 million messages. Zero failures.

9. Consumer Chapter Closed

v1.1.0 is the final Consumer release.

No further updates, bug fixes, enhancements, or refinements are planned for the Consumer/Sender side. The blackbox of SAP ADK Custom Adapter development is open. The code is public. The architecture is documented. The stress validation is done.

What the community has now:

  • A working Kafka Consumer adapter for SAP CPI — free and open source
  • mTLS, SASL/PLAIN/SCRAM, SASL_SSL, PLAINTEXT — all validated
  • TIMESEEK — single record and read-all-from-timestamp — fixed and validated
  • OSGi Relay fix — redeploy without port changes
  • 4.5M messages validated on SAP BTP Trial
  • 29 demo iFlows ready to import
  • Full source code, documentation, local Kafka demo environment

Next: EventSmartKafka Producer/Receiver Adapter.

Same architecture. Same reliability standard. Kafka producer role inside SAP CPI — cloud and on-premise.

10. A Note of Gratitude — SAP BTP Trial Infrastructure

Before closing this chapter, a sincere word of appreciation.

None of this validation would have been possible without the SAP BTP Trial infrastructure — and the team behind it deserves recognition. The machines are powerful, reliable, and remarkably generous for an independent solo researcher working outside any corporate budget.

This project ran two major validation phases entirely on Trial:

The first pillar of SDIA — Semantic Domain Integration Architecture — was the GDCR and DDCR validation: JavaScript Phantom v12 routing 40 endpoints to 4 proxies to 40 iFlows, delivering 1.5M+ messages — zero failures — across a complex multi-layer routing topology that pushed the Trial runtime well beyond typical usage patterns.

  • SAP BTP - API Management with SDIA — GDCR & DDCR in Practice
  • SAP BTP - APIM - DDCR - Deterministic Routing Mechanism JavaScript Policy full explanation each step

Github:

Phantom V12 - JavaScript Policy: https://github.com/rhviana/deip/blob/main/src/javascript/js-codes/dcrp-js-phantomv12.js

The second pillar was EventSmartKafka: 4.5 million messages total, with 3 million in 27 minutes under 4 simultaneous Cloud Connector tunnels, mTLS, SASL, SASL_SSL, and Avro payloads up to 2 MB — 3 GB transfered , all on the same Trial tenant, on a Sunday morning, with zero failures and zero CPI monitoring alerts.

Two pillars of SDIA. 6 million messages combined. Zero failures. On a Trial tenant. Free of charge. That says everything about the quality of the SAP BTP infrastructure team's work.

Congratulations and thank you to the SAP BTP infrastructure and platform engineering teams. The stability, the throughput capacity, and the runtime quality of SAP Cloud Integration — even on Trial — made it possible to deliver open-source research at a level that would otherwise require enterprise infrastructure.

This is what a great platform looks like in practice: it empowers independent researchers, students, and community contributors to build, validate, and publish real engineering work — without budget, without sponsors, and without compromise on quality.

Thank you, SAP.

Resources

 

ResourceURL
Repositoryhttps://github.com/rhviana/sap-ci-opensource-adapters
ESA Releasecustom-kafka/eventSmartKafka (.esa) releases
Demo iFlowscustom-kafka/pkg-iflows-sales-otc
Part ISAP BTP - CI - How I Built an Open-Source Enterprise Custom Kafka Adapter from Scratch - Part I
Part IISAP BTP - CI - Building Open-Source Custom Adapter EventSmartKafka from Zero with SAP ADK - Part II
Part IIISAP BTP - CI - EventSmartKafka - Custom Kafka Adapter From .ESA - Validated Runtime - Part III
SDIA Architecturehttps://zenodo.org/records/11185292
Author ORCIDhttps://orcid.org/0009-0009-9549-5862

Ricardo Viana
Integration Architecture Independent Solo Researcher
SAP BTP Integration Suite Expert Developer
SAP Press Author - Enterprise Messaging - 2021
Creator of DEIP · SDIA · GDCR · DDCR · ODCP · EDCP · DDCP

#SAPIntegrationSuite #SAPCloudIntegration #Kafka #SAPADK #OpenSource #ApacheKafka #CloudConnector #mTLS #OSGi #EventDrivenArchitecture #SDIA #StressTest #EnterpriseIntegration #SAPSCN

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities including agentic capabilities is now available!
entry.content:
@mio_yasutake For the side-by-side approach, both of the steps you mentioned are not required.

Regarding the "Login has failed: ... 409 Conflict" error, this can occur during the Joule logon flow if the Joule tenant activation in CBC has not yet been completed. It would be worth verifying that the CBC activation steps have been successfully finished.

Additionally, a 403 error is typically observed when the j4d_abap-enterprise-user group assignment is missing.

entry.title: Re: CK11N Ignores Fixed Quantity Indicator for BOM Component
entry.content: In SAP ECC, CK11N does not honor the “Fixed Quantity” indicator in BOMs during cost estimation — this is a known limitation. The system always multiplies component quantities by the costing lot size, even if the BOM item is flagged as fixed. There is no standard configuration to change this behavior; the workaround is either to adjust costing lot size or use alternative modeling approaches.

entry.title: Re: Aging of Revenue / VAT
entry.content:
Good day @mbanggo 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, there is no standard report that directly ages the VAT and revenue G/L lines based on whether the related customer invoice remains outstanding.

Standard receivables reporting evaluates the customer open item. For example, the Manage Customer Line Items app finds and reports customer line items, while Overdue Receivables analyses overdue customer receivables.

The closest standard analytical option is G/L Perspective or AR Aging Grid-Query, technical name C_ARAgingGridGenLedgerQry. It provides overdue and future receivables based on the mandatory company code and the P_KeyDate parameter and can be used with the Custom Analytical Queries app. However, its documented purpose remains receivables aging. It does not document independent againg of the original revenue and VAT lines.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/690ed2646ad649598c1184da312c260f.html?locale=en-US

What I recommend is:

  1. Test C_ARAgingGridGenLedgerQry first to determine whether its available G/L dimensions meet the client’s reporting requirement.
  2. Use Display Line Items in General Ledger to analyse postings to the relevant revenue and output VAT accounts.
  3. If the standard query is insufficient, create a custom analytical model with the Custom CDS Views app. It is recommended to use the released Journal Entry Item Browser or Journal Entry Item CDS view for journal entry reporting.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Getting Started with ihdlfscli – An Interactive CLI for SAP HANA Data Lake Files
entry.content:
Introduction

If you work with SAP HANA Data Lake Files (HDLFS), managing files, permissions, and connections can involve quite a bit of manual effort. ihdlfscli is an interactive command-line tool that makes this easier — think of it as an FTP-style shell for HDLFS, with auto-completion, multiple connection support, and coverage of most HDLFS features.

In this post, I'll walk you through installing ihdlfscli and using it to manage your HDLFS files.

Step 1: Install ihdlfscli

Download the latest release from:
https://github.tools.sap/tc/tools/releases/tag/ihdlfscli_2.1.9

Mac OS note: You may see a security warning when opening it. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security, scroll down to the Security section, and trust the binary. Then re-open it.

Step 2: Connect to Your HDLFS Instance

ihdlfscli supports several certificate types for authentication:

Type Format

PEM key + certkey.pem + cert.pem
P12*.p12 + passphrase
JKS*.jks + passphrase
JWT tokenjwt <token-value>
Sharable certificate- <cert-id>

To add a named environment (saved connection):

env add my-hdl 123456789.files.hdl.sap.hanacloud.ondemand.com /path/key.pem /path/cert.pem

Then connect to it:

env use my-hdl

For a one-off connection without saving:

env conn 123456789.files.hdl.sap.hanacloud.ondemand.com /path/key.pem /path/cert.pem

Step 3: File Operations

Once connected, you can work with your HDLFS files using familiar FTP-style commands.

Browse files:

ls
ls /mydata
pwd
cd /mydata

Upload and download:

put /local/file.csv /remote/data/
get /remote/data/file.csv /local/

Copy, rename, remove:

cp /data/a.txt /data/b.txt
rename /data/old.txt /data/new.txt
rm /data/old.txt

Search with regular expressions:

ls /data/^report.*
find /data .csv$

Check disk usage:

du /data 2

Tip: Auto-completion

Type any command and press Tab to get suggestions for sub-commands, remote paths, and options. This is especially handy when navigating deep directory structures.

What's Next?

ihdlfscli also covers policies, delta sharing, JWT management, and more. Check the full documentation at:
https://pages.github.tools.sap/tc/tools/hdlfs/ihdlfscli/index.html

entry.title: Re: SAP S4/HANA Cloud Public J78 Cash Flow reconciliation
entry.content:
Hi,

I encountered this problem as well, and thought of sharing how I managed to resolve it. 

For the Bank Statement Flows to have line items, make sure all the relevant settings of the bank for the intraday statement has been setup. Then, when creating a sample bank statement, use the file with format MT942. Here's the sample content:

Sample Code

:20:20180629123000 
:25:DRESDEFF827/2580061 
:28C:1 
:34F:EUR0, 
:13D:1806290000+0100 
:61:1806290629DR1120,00NTRF2000000169 
:86:062?00internaltest?20banktransfer?21interest 
:90D:1EUR1120,00

For further steps, try to check the test script for scope item 1EG.

Hope this helps.

 

Regards,

Jessie

entry.title: Re: CK11N Ignores Fixed Quantity Indicator for BOM Component
entry.content:
In SAP ECC, the “Fixed Quantity” indicator in the bill of materials ensures a constant quantity of raw materials during production (for example, in CS11 or CO01), regardless of the quantity of finished goods. However, standard cost estimation (CK11N) does not take this setting into account and multiplies the component quantities by the quantity of finished goods, resulting in incorrect cost calculations.

  • The “Fixed Quantity” indicator functions as expected in production but is ignored in CK11N.
    A costing lot size of 1 does not override the quantity calculation logic in CK11N.
    There is no standard SAP setting to enforce fixed quantities in CK11N.
  • Manually adjust component quantities in the costing records or use substitution rules.
    Implement custom exits or alternative costing methods if strictly fixed quantities are required.

This behavior is standard in SAP ECC, and AFAIK there is no native solution that allows CK11N to take the “Fixed Quantity” indicator into account. Workarounds may be necessary.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

entry.title: Re: Transitioning from CSE/HR to SAP: Should I learn RAP and BTP directly?
entry.content:
Hi Chaitania,

thank you for your question and your interest in learning RAP!

I believe that your Computer Science background should enable you to get into RAP without the necessity of mastering core ABAP. Taken into consideration that you are familiar with Object Oriented Concepts, you should be able to at least follow and understand the ABAP code implementation in RAP especially in Managed Scenarios and the simple EML examples.* 

I would suggest to start with the documentation: ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model . This would allow you to get a high level overview for RAP and its architecture, show you some best practices and make you familiar with RAP Stack terminology. In addition, you have some concrete examples of different RAP Business Objects implementations. The documentation itself explains in detail the code provided in these examples.

To sum it up, this should be a very good start to get familiar with RAP.  My suggestion is: always experiment directly with some example in parallel. Learning by doing should allow you to get a better feeling about the issues and the how-to. Start with this, implement some simple examples and of course, you can always aim to improve and master the ABAP skills in the next steps.

Good luck and have fun!

Kind Regards,
Vlora

* For your information, the Entity Manipulation Language is a part of the ABAP language that enables access to RAP business objects and Managed RAP Business Object is the type of RAP Implementation where most of implementation is overtaken by the framework.

entry.title: Changing "supported by" of our SAP systems in SAP for Me
entry.content:
My company had to switch with the SAP licenses to our owner in Japan. Therefor we had to cancel the contract with our system house. Now we need to raise an incident according to one of our productive SAP-Systems, but we cannot do this, as the systems-house is still in the supported by data. Our parent company told us, that we should switch the "supported by" to my company, as they have it the way too. So, how can we replace the "supported by" company to ourselves?

I spent hours of searching for an solution but was not able to find anything usefull (as we cannot raise a case in SAP for me, because it requires to select one of our SAP systems, which cannot be done, because the system house is still the "supported by ").

Thanks a lot in advance.

entry.title: CPI With Azure Bucket
entry.content:
We have noticed that Azure Storage occasionally changes the bucket ID. When this happens, the integration stops working until I perform a connectivity test without credentials to retrieve the new bucket ID. After that, I have to update the known.hosts file with the new ID before the connection works again.

Is there a recommended approach to avoid this issue? Specifically, is it possible to configure the connection without relying on the bucket ID, or is there a more stable authentication or configuration method that prevents this from happening?

entry.title: HCM Brasil – Crédito do Trabalhador: Dedução de Garantia na Rescisão – Entrega Concluída!
entry.content:
Boa tarde, pessoal!

Na última sexta-feira, concluímos as entregas relacionadas ao novo cálculo do Crédito do Trabalhador no módulo HCM, em atendimento à Portaria MTE Nº 1.115, de 25 de junho de 2026, que estabelece a utilização de garantias nas operações de crédito com consignação em folha de pagamento.

O que representa essa entrega?

Com a publicação da portaria, os empregadores passam a ser obrigados a deduzir um valor de garantia de empréstimo das verbas rescisórias do empregado no momento da rescisão do contrato, para cada contrato ativo de Crédito do Trabalhador.

Para atender a essa exigência, o módulo HCM passa a:

  • Registrar os contratos de Crédito do Trabalhador com rescisão no Infotipo 3453 – subtipo 0002 (Terminação).
  • Calcular automaticamente a dedução de garantia na folha de rescisão (motivo RESC), respeitando a fórmula: min(percVerbaRescisoriaGarantia × Remuneração Disponível, valorSaldoDevedor).
  • Armazenar a Remuneração Disponível na nova rubrica /ART, composta pelas rubricas configuradas na visão V_T596J - subaplicação PIDW.
  • Gerar as rubricas de dedução com natRubr = 9253, para serem reportadas nos eventos S-2299 e S-2399.

SAP Notes liberadas

Aplique as SAP Notes na seguinte sequência:

  1. 3767464 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Notes 3767532 and 3767531
  2. 3767532 - Crédito do trabalhador – Infotype 3453 – New subtype 0002: Worker credit termination
  3. 3773474 - Crédito do trabalhador – Loan Guarantee Deduction on Termination Payroll
  4. 3769777 - Crédito do trabalhador – Guidance for customizing the Available Remuneration for Termination Payroll

Para clientes HR Renewal:

  • 3767531 - Crédito do trabalhador – HR Renewal – Infotype 3453 – New subtype 0002: Worker credit termination

Maiores Informações

Para mais informações e documentação completa sobre este tema, acesse a página dedicada no Workzone: 👉 Crédito do Trabalhador – Dedução de Garantia na Rescisão

Gostou deste conteúdo? Deixe seu like, compartilhe com colegas e registre dúvidas, experiências ou sugestões nos comentários! Siga a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para se manter sempre atualizado.

Um grande abraço!

Anderson Marques

what errors do you get where?  I can't see any screenshot mentioned by you.
In 7.1 the workflow will only work documents that use a digital signature and of course only when signature process needs at least two signees to sign to be 
finished an set it end status. 

When a new approval process is initiated by the status change system does trigger the event SIGNATURE_PROCESS_START of the business object SOLMANDOC

When this prerequisite is fulfilled one could create own workflow in SWDD for  business object SOLMANDOC
 regards; 

thomas

entry.title: "Country/region is not active in your system" when maintaining RITA countries in SSCUI 103464
entry.content:
When attempting to maintain a foreign tax registration country/region in SSCUI 103464 (Activate RITA and Maintain Tax Registration Countries/Regions), the following error may occur:

FOT_TXA080 – Country/region <XX> is not active in your system.

In some cases, the additional message "RITA cannot be activated for country/region *" may also be displayed, even though the country/region is listed as supported for Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: "Country/region is not active in your system" when ma...
entry.content:
This behavior is expected if the requested country/region content is not active in the system.

Support for a country/region within Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA) does not, by itself, indicate that the corresponding country/region content is active in the tenant.

Before a country/region can be maintained as a foreign tax registration country in SSCUI 103464, ensure that the corresponding country/region content has been activated in accordance with the RITA prerequisites and the applicable Central Business Configuration (CBC) requirements.

The activation requirements may differ depending on the country/region and supported business scenario. Refer to SAP Note 2907372 and the Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad documentation for the applicable prerequisites.

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management Not E...
entry.content: Employee Central is not required for Job Selector functionality. The non-EC version uses a four-part menu structure (Family / Role / Code / Grade) that an administrator maintains across three tables.  These fields must be maintained in you back end tables and included in your template.  You should make sure comp profile is enabled as well.

entry.title: Demystifying Model Context Protocol (MCP), Part 3: Integration with Joule
entry.content:
Recap and Final Steps

In Part 2, we made the concepts concrete by building a commerce MCP server using FastMCP, exposing agent‑ready tools that wrap Commerce (CAP‑based) OData APIs. You saw how to run the server locally, configure destinations and deploy the MCP server on the cloud. By the end of Part 2, you had a fully running commerce MCP server, with tools like get_products and get_retailers discoverable and testable via an MCP inspector.

In this final Part 3, we will move from a standalone MCP server to a true end‑to‑end agentic experience by integrating it with an MCP client in SAP Joule. Building on the same commerce MCP server, we will use Joule Studio to:

  1. Create a content based Joule Assistant
  2. Understand Prompt Engineering
  3. Configure the Commerce MCP server (Built in Part 2)
  4. Test and release the Joule Assistant

By the end of this series, you will have an end to end working model for an enterprise use case to design, build an MCP server and use it in Joule Agent.

Pre-requisites:

  1. You should have access to SAP Build
  2. You should have access to joule studio
  3. You should be able to configure destination in destination service

Let's get started.

Architecture and Building Blocks

The following solution diagram illustrates the main components of the application from a agent creator / developer perspective.

The main components of the above diagram are explained below

  1. The central entry point for building custom Joule skills and AI agents created is Joule Studio. Joule Studio is part of SAP Build, which includes SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Code, and SAP Build Process Automation. It leverages SAP Build's user experience and lifecycle management capabilities.
  2. Joule Studio enables extending Joule by creating new capabilities that can be deployed alongside the out-of-the-box capabilities provided by SAP. These capabilities include Joule Skills and AI Agents. 
  3. AI Core plays an important role in extending Joule's capabilities. It provides the underlying Large Language Models (LLMs) that can be leveraged to configure AI Agents. 
  4. SAP BTP Connectivity integrates AI Agents with SAP and non-SAP systems. This gives access to business data which is important to provide context to the LLMs.
  5. Once developers have created their custom Joule Skills and AI Agents, they can use SAP Build's lifecycle capabilities to compile and deploy these capabilities. This grants business users access to additional functionalities, helping them in their daily tasks through centralized access via Joule, reducing the need to switch between multiple SAP and non-SAP applications.
  6. Joule Studio relies on SAP Cloud Identity Services for identity management, authentication and identity life-cycle management. SAP Cloud Identity Services act as a central facade for identity and access management, offering secure authentication or federation with third-party identity providers. 

Hands-on

Step-1: Configure Destination For Remote MCP Server

The first thing Joule needs is a BTP destination that points to your deployed commerce MCP server. Joule Studio discovers MCP servers exclusively through HTTP destinations that are marked as MCP-capable.

  1. In the SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your Joule Studio subaccount, then go to Connectivity - Destinations - New Destination.
  2. Create an HTTP destination that points to the root URL of your commerce MCP server (for example, the Cloud Foundry route of your FastMCP app). Do not append /mcp to the URL here; Joule Studio will add the MCP path later when you configure the server in the agent.
  3. Choose an appropriate Authentication type for your MCP server (for example, NoAuthentication for a simple lab setup, or OAuth2ClientCredentials if your MCP server is protected by XSUAA). Fill the client ID, client secret, and token service URL from the corresponding service key of your XSUAA instance when using OAuth.
  4. Add the mandatory additional property so Joule can recognize this destination as an MCP server:
    1. Property: sap-joule-studio-mcp-server
    2. Value: true
  5. Save the destination and click Check Connection to verify that BTP can reach your MCP server endpoint.

Step-2: Joule Studio Project Set Up

With the BTP destination in place, the next step is to set up a Joule Studio project and agent that will later consume the commerce MCP server.

Pre-requisites:

  1. From the SAP Build lobby, open the Control Tower tile.
  2. Go to the Environments section and create the environment where you plan to run your Joule agent later.
  3. Next, Go to Destination section and Add new destination. You should see the destination in the dropdown list which you configured in step-1. This is because of the property: sap-joule-studio-mcp-server.

Create Joule Studio Project:

  1. From the SAP Build lobby, create a new project of type - Joule Agent and Skill
  2. Add Name: "Commerce AI Project"and Description: "This project consists of e-commerce AI assistant agents"
  3. Once the project is created, Open the project. Inside it, create "Joule Agent". Give Name, Identifier and Description. Refer screenshot below 

Step-3: Prompt Engineering

For Step 3, you can focus on how to “teach” the Joule agent to use the commerce MCP tools reliably.

Now that the Joule project and environment are ready, the next crucial step is to engineer the agent’s brain through its instructions. In Joule Studio, this lives in the Expertise & Instructions section and acts as the system prompt that guides how the agent reasons, when it calls MCP tools, and how it responds back to the user. I am giving the example below

  • Expertise: 
You are a commerce assistant that helps business users discover products, check availability, and find retailers using the commerce MCP tools available to you.​
  • Instructions: 
PURPOSE AND OBJECTIVE:
The E-Commerce AI Assistant is designed to assist the user with any e-commerce related questions using the configured MCP tools.

PROCESS:
1. Analyze the user's query to determine the specific information or action requested (e.g., categories, products, products by category, or retailers).
2. Invoke only the relevant tools that matches the user's request. For example:
   - If the user asks for categories, invoke the tool to retrieve categories only.
   - If the user requests products, invoke the tool to retrieve the product list.
   - If the user requests products by category, first invoke the tool to get the categories. Then from the category response, get the relevant category id to fetch the products related to the category.
   - If the user requests retailers, invoke the tool to retrieve retailer information.
3. Do not fetch additional data if not asked by the user. If the user asks product information, do not get any additional information about retailers or orders etc. 
4. Fetch only the data which is required to provide answer to the user.
5. Format and present the output in a clear and structured manner for the user.
6. If any required information is missing or an error occurs, ask the user for clarification or report the issue clearly.

TOOLING:
- Use the configured MCP server to fetch tools to retrieve relevant data from the MCP server.
- Ensure all communication is clear and concise, and handle errors gracefully.​
  • Additional Context:
Please maintain a clear, professional, and supportive tone. This agent is designed to assist e-commerce users and operations teams in understanding available products and categories. Also provide retailers who can fulfill the products which the user is interested in.

Please hide informations like - ID, tax id or SSN from the response.

Recommendations should be practical, action-oriented, and phrased respectfully, especially when issues are detected. The agent must avoid vague language. If data is unavailable, it should state so explicitly and guide the user accordingly. 

The overall voice should reflect operational reliability, transparency, and collaboration, aligning with values of efficiency, accountability, and continuous improvement.

Next is to provide the LLM model settings of your choice. I have used Open AI models for planning and response. You can configure Agent Execution Steps based on your preference.

Step-4: Configuring MCP Server

  1. Click Add MCP Server.
  2. In the dialog, provide a Name such as Commerce MCP Server so that it is clear which backend domain this server represents.
  3. For BTP Destination, select the HTTP destination you created earlier (for example, COMMERCE_MCP_DESTINATION) that already has sap-joule-studio-mcp-server=true.
  4. Once you select the destination, you will see the list of tools exposed by the MCP server.
  5. Click Add Server.
  6. Confirm and save. 

Step-5: Test and Release the Joule Assistant

Once the MCP server is configured in Joule Studio, the final step is to validate the assistant in a private environment and then release it for use. This is where you confirm that the agent can interpret prompts correctly, call the commerce MCP tools, and return grounded responses instead of generic answers.

During testing, check three things carefully:
  1. The agent chooses the right MCP tool for the user intent.
  2. The tool response is summarized clearly and accurately in natural language.
  3. Missing or ambiguous inputs trigger a clarifying question instead of a wrong tool call or a hallucinated answer.
If the behavior is not what you expect, go back to the prompt instructions and refine the agent’s goals, tool guidance, or response style. In practice, prompt tuning is usually an iterative step, a small wording change can significantly improve the overall output.
 
After the testing looks solid, release the agent to the private environment so it can run as a usable Joule assistant. Once you click Release, Go back to Control Tower - Joule Assistants. In the Standalone Assistants section, you will see your custom joule assistant. Open the joule assistant and start the conversation. 

 

What we built so far ?

By the end of this blog series, we built a Joule assistant for commerce scenarios that is powered by a remote MCP server. The setup connects a BTP destination, a private runtime environment, and a Joule Studio Agent so the assistant can discover and call commerce tools instead of answering generically.

The end result is an end-to-end flow where a user prompt goes into Joule, Joule uses the MCP tools exposed by your server, and the assistant returns grounded commerce data such as products, categories, and retailers.
 
In other words, you turned a standalone MCP server into a usable enterprise AI assistant inside Joule Studio.

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://architecture.learning.sap.com/docs/ref-arch/06ff6062dc/3
  2. https://help.sap.com/docs/Joule_Studio/45f9d2b8914b4f0ba731570ff9a85313/193461e9d8494995bab4889b22afac93.html

entry.title: "First posting item is not in leading company code &" when posting intercompany journal entries
entry.content:
When posting an intercompany journal entry using the Post General Journal Entries app, the following error may be raised during simulation or posting:

F5A296 – First posting item is not in leading company code &.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: "First posting item is not in leading company code &&"...
entry.content:
For manual postings using the Post General Journal Entries app, the company code entered in the document header must match the company code of the first line item. If they differ, message F5A296 is issued.

To resolve the issue, ensure that the first line item belongs to the same company code as the document header. If necessary, reorder the journal entry line items so that the first line item matches the header company code.

entry.title: Re: Salea header text in Quotation
entry.content: Did you check in VOTXN in which Texts for both document header and item would be configured and this should be assigned to your quotation document type

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Bonus worksheet - Negative record in the employee history- Incorrect finalPayout Ca
entry.content: this seems messy because in your example, you have overlapping assignments with one positive salary and another negative salary. In scenarios like this, one possible solution is to use the earnings table. This takes salary out of the equation and instead you upload a file of earnings where you can control the number used in the calculation and you don't need to add unnecessary assignments.
